author: maxine--futura
content:
Findings from /qa review of .claude/hooks/ and .crosslink/ changes (953 insertions, 275 deletions across 10 files):

**CRITICAL (3):**
1. hook-config.json type mutations — auto_steal_stale_locks changed bool→string, reminder_drift_threshold int→string, tracker_remote set to placeholder '(text)'
2. session-start.py has its own run_crosslink() instead of importing from crosslink_config.py — binary discovery divergence
3. .gitignore bootstrapping paradox — .claude/hooks/ and .claude/commands/ changed from tracked to gitignored but still tracked in git

**WARNING (8):**
- session-start.py makes 12+ sequential subprocess calls (4 redundant session status calls), risks 10s hook timeout
- build_reminder() has 10 parameters (God Function signature)
- load_all_rules() returns bare 5-tuple instead of NamedTuple
- build_design_context() has cyclomatic complexity ~12 with copy-paste fallback blocks
- get_lock_file_hash() is dead code (never called)
- estimate_prompt_chars uses hardcoded 5x multiplier — context budget feature is unreliable
- import re inside function bodies instead of module-level
- Unused datetime/timezone import in session-start.py

**NITPICK (3):**
- has_active_session() explicit True/False instead of idiomatic bool()
- interval==0 semantics unclear in condensed reminder logic
- Lint debounce logic inlined in main() instead of extracted
